In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "But you will answer that you abide by the same declaration, ‘He that is once washed needeth not save to wash his feet.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Now the ‘once’ is once that has authority, once that is confirmed by the truth."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: Baptism in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost has Christ for its authority, not any man, whoever he may be; and Christ is the truth, not any man.
